title: Oeceoclades zanzibarica
taxonomy: Oeceoclades zanzibarica is a species of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the African terrestrial orchids. This genus is part of the subtribe Angraecinae and is native to tropical regions of East Africa. The classification of Oeceoclades zanzibarica is as follows: kingdom Plantae, clade Angiosperms, clade Monocots, order Asparagales, family Orchidaceae, subtribe Angraecinae, genus Oeceoclades, species O. zanzibarica. intro: Oeceoclades zanzibarica, also known as the Zanzibar orchid or greenhood orchid, is a species of terrestrial orchid that belongs to the genus Oeceoclades. The plant has been traditionally used in Eastern Africa for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin infections due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The bioactive constituents of Oeceoclades zanzibarica include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is commonly used to make herbal teas and infusions for its medicinal uses.
